<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen_skill_docs.py — do not edit by hand. -->

# Events & reaction policy

Two event surfaces (see `get_state`):

- **`reflexes_recent`** — a rolling buffer of *hazard* reflex fires. These react instantly via the registry; you observe them after the fact.
- **`events`** — a flushable buffer of everything else (chat / death / respawn + the mod's world-mutation log). `get_state(flush=True)` drains it; `wait_for_event` blocks on it.

Install your own reaction body for any event type with `set_handler` (see handlers.md). The defaults below are what runs until you do.

## Hazard events (react via the reflex registry)

| event | preempts | cooldown_s | resumes | default reaction |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `damage_taken` | False | 30 | True | hostile mob → retaliate (HP>6) or flee (HP≤6); fall/fire/player/phantom → record-only |
| `entered_lava` | True | 5 | True | walk to nearest shore |
| `started_drowning` | True | 10 | True | surface, then walk to shore |
| `tool_broke` | True | 1 | True | record-only (you decide whether to re-equip) |
| `hostile_nearby` | False | 3 | False | awareness only — never preempts, never wakes you |

## Other events (recorded to the flushable buffer)

| event | default preempts | behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `death` | True | the bot died — preempts the action slot, then recorded |
| `chat` | False | a player said something — recorded only |
| `respawn` | False | recorded only |

Plus `reflex_done` (synthesized when a hazard handler finishes recovering) and the mod's world-mutation events (`block_broken`, `block_placed`, `entity_attacked`) which arrive via `get_state(flush=True)` with `{type, data, ts}`.
